messages.content:
whats the right answer
____________________ not only determine the charge of an ion, but the bonds the atom is capable of making.

Core electrons
Valence electrons
Protons
Neutrons
The right answer is Valence electrons. Valence electrons not only determine the charge of an ion but also the type of bonds that an atom is capable of making.

fill the blanks
In an ionic compound, a metal and a __________(a)__________ must have charges that equal to _________(b)____________.

using this word bank
Zero	1  	positive	negative	nonmetal	noble gas
In an ionic compound, a metal and a nonmetal must have charges that equal to zero.

fill in the blank
____________________ are an element family that represents that stability that ions are trying to achieve.
Noble gases are an element family that represents the stability that ions are trying to achieve.
provide the number of protons in an aluminum atom:
The number of protons in an aluminum atom is 13.
provide the number of neutrons in an aluminum atom:
The number of neutrons in an aluminum atom is 14.
provide the mass in an aluminum atom
The mass of an aluminum atom is approximately 27 atomic mass units (amu).
provide the number of electrons in a neutral aluminum atom:
The number of electrons in a neutral aluminum atom is also 13.
Choose the correct charge of the aluminum ion:
The correct charge of the aluminum ion is +3.

Choose the ions that can create an ionic compound with the aluminum ion

Li-3 N-7  O-8  Si-14 P-15
The ions that can create an ionic compound with the aluminum ion are Li-3 and O-8.
what is the ion name of a electron shell model with 9 protons 2 electrons on the inner shell and 8 on the outer shell
The ion name of an electron shell model with 9 protons, 2 electrons on the inner shell, and 8 on the outer shell would be fluorine (F-) ion.
what is the name of an atom with 9 protons 2 electrons on the inner shell and 7 on the outermost
The name of an atom with 9 protons, 2 electrons on the inner shell, and 7 electrons on the outermost shell is fluorine (F).
Choose the correct charge of the ion:
I'm sorry, but you haven't provided any options for me to choose the correct charge of the ion. Could you please provide the options?
of flourine with 9 protons 2 electrons on the inner shell and 8 on the outermost
The correct charge of the ion for fluorine with 9 protons, 2 electrons on the inner shell, and 8 electrons on the outermost shell is -1.

Choose the correct statement(s) that explains how an ion becomes charged
*

Atoms that gain electrons become negatively charged.
Atoms that gain electrons become positively charged.
Atoms that lose electrons become positively charged.
Atoms that lose electrons become negatively charged.

The correct statements that explain how an ion becomes charged are:

- Atoms that gain electrons become negatively charged.
- Atoms that lose electrons become positively charged.
correct chemical name for PtCl_2
The correct chemical name for PtCl₂ is platinum(II) chloride.
correct chemical formula for copper (III) phosphate
The correct chemical formula for copper (III) phosphate is CuPO₄.
Choose the ions that can create an ionic compound with the aluminum ion:
The ions that can create an ionic compound with the aluminum ion are typically those with a charge of -1, such as Cl- (chloride), O2- (oxide), and S2- (sulfide).
can Li-3 and O-8  create an ionic compound with the aluminum ion:
Yes, Li-3 (lithium) and O-8 (oxide) can create an ionic compound with the aluminum ion (Al3+).

1) Most Reactive	(1) Least Reactive	(2) Gains e-(s)	(2) Loses e-(s)	(3) Metal	(3) Nonmetal	(4) Cation	(4) Anion
Na								
Cl								
Na								
Cl

1) Most Reactive - Na (sodium) (3) Metal
2) Least Reactive - Cl (chlorine) (3) Nonmetal
Gains e-(s) - Cl (chlorine) (2) Gains e-(s) (4) Anion
Loses e-(s) - Na (sodium) (2) Loses e-(s) (4) Cation
Is NaCl an ionic compound?
Yes, NaCl is an ionic compound. It is formed by the ionic bonding between the sodium cation (Na+) and the chloride anion (Cl-).

(1) Most Reactive	(1) Least Reactive	(2) Gains e-(s)	(2) Loses e-(s)	(3) Metal	(3) Nonmetal	(4) Cation	(4) Anion

Mg

1) Most Reactive - Mg (magnesium) (3) Metal
1) Least Reactive - O (oxygen) (3) Nonmetal
2) Gains e-(s) - O (oxygen) (2) Gains e-(s) (4) Anion
2) Loses e-(s) - Mg (magnesium) (2) Loses e-(s) (4) Cation
Is MgO an ionic compound?
Yes, MgO is an ionic compound. It is formed by the ionic bonding between the magnesium cation (Mg2+) and the oxide anion (O2-).

1) Most Reactive - C (carbon) (3) Nonmetal
1) Least Reactive - H (hydrogen) (3) Nonmetal
1) Reactivity Unknown - C (carbon) (3) Nonmetal
2) Gains e-(s) - H (hydrogen) (2) Gains e-(s) (4) Anion
2) Loses e-(s) - C (carbon) (2) Loses e-(s) (4) Cation
2) Electron Movement Unknown - C (carbon)
3) Metal - N/A
3) Nonmetal - C (carbon), H (hydrogen)
